title: At the party
setting: At Lukas's party, someone you haven't met turns to you.

steps.speaker: Nina
steps.es: Sali! Dich han ich no nie gseh. Wär bisch du?
steps.en: Hi! I've never seen you before. Who are you?

options.es: Sali! Ich bi de Tom, e Fründ vom Lukas.
options.en: Hi! I'm Tom, a friend of Lukas.
options.feedback: Perfect — name plus how you fit in. That's exactly how to place yourself at a party.
Gives your name but little to grab onto. Mentioning Lukas would open the chat.

options.en: Why do you want to know?
options.feedback: Way too guarded at a friendly party — she's just being sociable.

steps.speaker: Nina
steps.es: Ah schön! Ich bi d'Nina. Wohär chunsch?
steps.en: Ah nice! I'm Nina. Where are you from?

options.es: Us Amerika. Ich wohne sit es halbs Jahr da.
options.en: From America. I've lived here for half a year.
options.feedback: Great — gives her two things to ask about. Conversation flows from detail.
Clear and fine. A touch more detail would invite more chat.
Playful, but can fall flat with someone you just met. Keep it easy.

steps.speaker: Nina
steps.en: And how do you like Lucerne?

options.es: Sehr guet! D'Lüt sind fründlich und de See isch traumhaft.
options.en: Very much! The people are friendly and the lake is dreamlike.
options.feedback: Warm and specific — locals love hearing what you appreciate about home.
Perfectly nice, if a little brief.
Lukewarm — and a Lucerner might take it personally. A little warmth goes far.

steps.speaker: Nina
steps.es: Schön, dass d'da bisch! Mir gsehnd öis sicher wider.
steps.en: Glad you're here! We'll surely see each other again.

options.es: Das hoff ich! Es het mich gfreut, Nina.
options.en: I hope so! Nice to meet you, Nina.
options.feedback: Warm and personal, using her name. A great first impression.
A bit noncommittal after a warm chat. Match her openness.
